reasoning: They spoke on May 8, just before the May 12 resolution window opened, discussing Victory Day and a 30-day ceasefire. The recent timing of this call (just 4 days prior to the start of the window) somewhat decreases the likelihood of another call early in the window. However, the 30-day timeframe mentioned aligns exactly with early June. Furthermore, the G7 Summit starts on June 15 (just after the window closes). It is common for leaders to hold a coordination call prior to a major summit, but it's also possible they wait to speak until they see each other in person. Because they recently communicated and have an upcoming in-person opportunity, the probability of an intervening phone call is balanced around 45%.
